context: Tests whether the agent correctly uses Shopify's metafield hooks for reading and writing checkout data, imports from the correct Shopify extension library, registers the extension against a valid checkout target, and produces a properly configured TOML file.

checklist.name: Correct import source
checklist.description: All hooks, components, and `reactExtension` are imported exclusively from `@shopify/ui-extensions-react/checkout` — no imports from `react-dom`, native HTML form elements, or other UI libraries

checklist.name: useApplyMetafieldsChange for saving
checklist.description: Uses the `useApplyMetafieldsChange` hook to persist the note text — does NOT use `useApplyCartLinesChange`, `fetch`, or `localStorage` for this purpose

checklist.name: useMetafield for reading
checklist.description: Uses the `useMetafield` hook (with a namespace and key) to read the existing note value for pre-population — does NOT derive the value from local state alone

checklist.name: updateMetafield operation type
checklist.description: Calls `applyMetafieldsChange` with `type: "updateMetafield"` and includes `namespace`, `key`, `valueType`, and `value` fields

checklist.name: No DOM/browser APIs
checklist.description: Does NOT reference `document`, `window`, `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, or other browser/DOM globals in the extension code

checklist.name: reactExtension entry point
checklist.description: Uses `reactExtension()` as the exported entry point wrapping the component

checklist.name: Valid checkout target in source
checklist.description: Registers against a valid checkout extension target string (e.g. `purchase.checkout.shipping-option-list.render-after`, `purchase.checkout.block.render`, or another documented target) — not an invented string

checklist.name: TOML api_version present
checklist.description: The `shopify.extension.toml` includes an `api_version` field with a version string

checklist.name: TOML target matches source
checklist.description: The `target` value in `[[extensions.targeting]]` in the TOML matches the target string passed to `reactExtension()` in the source file
